definition: Knowing arising dependent on tongue and taste.
notes: The project prefers tongue-knowing because it matches the canonical pattern while aligning with the updated treatment of viññāṇa as knowing. Tongue-consciousness remains an acceptable alternate where continuity with familiar doctrinal English matters.

context_rules.context: In most analytical and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing the sensory modality
In comparative doctrinal prose using established Buddhist-English terminology
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when continuity with standard terminology helps.

example_phrases.pali: jivhañca paṭicca rase ca uppajjati jivhāviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on the tongue and tastes, tongue-knowing arises
